themes.label: Peace/Rest
themes.summary: This chapter offers peace, rest, stillness, comfort, or settled safety in God. Local Strong’s word study and cross references data reinforce this reading across 4 verses. Commentary leans on "Then all the children of Israel, and all the people, went up, and came unto the house of God,...". Word-study detail reinforces "וּשְׁלָמִ֖ים: and peace offerings - properly, requital, i.e., a (voluntary) sacrifice in thanks".

themes.label: Covenant
themes.summary: This chapter points to covenant, promise, oath, inheritance, or the enduring faithfulness of God. Local Strong’s word study and cross references data reinforce this reading across 2 verses. Commentary leans on "I took my concubine, and cut her in pieces (וָאֹחֵז בְּפִילַגְשִׁי וָאֲנַתְּחֶהָ)—the verb 'c...". Word-study detail reinforces "בְּרִ֣ית: of the covenant - a compact (because made by passing between pieces of flesh)".
